headline: Slim Jim Maker Conagra Replacing CEO Following 60% Stock Slide
summary: (Bloomberg) -- Conagra Brands Inc. announced its chief executive, Sean Connolly, will step down at the end of next month and be replaced by consumer goods veteran John Brase, on June 1.Brase, 58, most recently served as the president and chief operating officer of JM Smucker Co., which he joined in 2020. He previously spent roughly 30 years at Procter & Gamble. At Smucker, Brase oversaw the company’s retail coffee, its frozen handheld products, including the booming Uncrustables business and pet

headline: Conagra Brands Appoints John Brase as President and Chief Executive Officer
summary: Conagra Brands, Inc. (NYSE: CAG), today announced that John Brase has been named President and Chief Executive Officer of the Company, effective June 1, 2026. Brase will also join Conagra's Board of Directors. He succeeds Sean Connolly, who will step away from his leadership roles and from the Board on May 31, 2026, after more than a decade of leadership.
